content: I can't imagine any supported mail client offering to support TLS 1.0, considering the security vulnerabilities
creator: wsmwk

content:
Wayne Mery said
I can't imagine any supported mail client offering to support TLS 1.0, considering the security vulnerabilities

I suspect that you are right. My email provider has created a partial but imperfect solution -- he created a proxy server that I can connect to at TLS 1.2 but connections to the main mail server are still at TLS 1.0. Another problem with that is that I don't receive some of my email because some servers return email rather than make an unsecure connection. He isn't inclined to fix the latter problem because of the complexity and I'm just a legacy user who has been grandfathered in after the company changed their business model. So, I guess I am stuck having to move to a new primary email account, anyway. <sigh>

content:
garyt said
Are there any work arounds? Does it work for anyone? Is what host we are on useful for diagnosis?

To answer my own question, hopefully for the benefit of anyone else that comes here - yes, there is. On github GerryArqIT suggested a workaround: disabling "Auto-detect IMAP namespace" and leaving IMAP path prefix empty, the problem is gone. Which is under settings/(account)/fetching mail/Incoming server Which works perfectly for me. And it looks like they are working on it.

content:
Unifying the spam folder would cause it's content to be displayed inside the Unified Inbox, not as a separate folder underneath it. I would advise against doing this, mixing spam and normal emails together.

A unified versions of the special folders i.e Spam, Trash, Draft etc is a feature the developers are planning to add in the future. It's not available yet.

See the link below. https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/8812?issue=thunderbird%7Cthunderbird-android%7C9370
creator: platform34

content: Hmm. I turned on the "Unify" option for my Gmail's spam folder, but I still don't see it under the unified account. Just the inbox.
creator: spartanhooah

content:
Hi spartanhooah

The folders previously displayed below Unified inbox were those associated with the selected email account.

They are still available by pressing one of the left hand circle icons. This displays the selected email account and it's folders.

Emails from folders (any account) with "Unify" enabled continue to be displayed within the Unified Inbox.

The design was changed to move the Unified Inbox under it's own Unified account header. It's part of ongoing development that will in time bring other Unified folders under the same header, such as Unified Sent, Unified Draft, Unified trash etc.

1. Thunderbird for Android does not have address book of its own, and Thunderbird for Android can use the address book of your Android phone.

2. You can post your feature request in the following forum: https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android

There is already the same feature request in the above forum, and you can upvote it: an address book in Thunderbird for android https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/an-address-book-in-thunderbird-for-android/idi-p/84952

content:
Hi,

You'll want to click the three horizontal lines at the top left corner of the app, then the gear at the bottom left corner.

Then, select your account (xxx@xxx.xxx) under "Accounts". Select "Reading Mail", then "Always Show Images". You should be able to choose to show images from contacts or from anyone.

Hope this helps! A
